title: System Design Case Study and Behavioral
learningObjectives: Apply **System design case study and behavioral** with production-grade tests, tools, and measurable outcomes
Explain trade-offs between test types: unit, slice, integration, contract, e2e
Use profiling and benchmarking vocabulary correctly in senior interviews
Deliver behavioral answers with STAR structure tied to real architecture decisions
Run mock interviews with follow-up depth on testing and performance
Connect reliability practices from earlier phases to capstone scenarios

sections.title: Why System Design Case Study and Behavioral matters
Theory and Internals - System Design Case Study and Behavioral
sections.content:
### Day 89 — **Case** **study** + **behavioral**

### 1. Clarify
**Requirements**, **scale**, **constraints**.

### 2. High level
**API**, **data**, **components**.

### 3. Deep dives
**Bottleneck**, **failure**, **security**.

### 4. Evolution
**Migrations**, **strangler**, **SLOs**.

### 5. Behavioral
**STAR** **stories** with **numbers**.

### 6. Story
**Monolith** **split**—**you** **proposed** **boundary** **and** **measured** **MTTR** **drop**.

sections.title: Basic — STAR letters
Intermediate — Trade-off framing
sections.output: option A cost vs option B latency vs option C ops complexity

sections.title: Advanced — Result with metric
sections.output: result: p99 down 40% and incident count halved next quarter

sections.title: Exercise — System Design Case Study and Behavioral (Day 89 assignment)
sections.problem:
Print **exactly** two lines:
1. Implemented: System Design Case Study + Behavioral
2. Focus: STAR response for architecture trade-off decision.

sections.title: Interview Drill

conceptual.question: **STAR** **mapping** for **architecture**?
conceptual.answer: Situation: constraints; Task: reliability goal; Action: design + metrics; Result: outage reduction or cost win. Explain **second-order** effects: **retry** amplification, **queue** growth, **thread** **pool** exhaustion, and **cost** of extra **hops** when **edge** dependencies like **Redis** rate-limit stores fail. Close with **prevention**: **contract** tests for **clients**, **SLO** alerts on **client-side** **error** rates, **game** days that **kill** **pods** during **business** hours in **staging**, and **lint** rules banning **blocking** calls on **reactor** threads.

followUps.question: Production follow-up: which **dashboards** or **metrics** expose this for **System design case study**?
followUps.answer: Start with **client** **outbound** **histograms** and **resilience4j** **circuitbreaker** **state** **gauges**, then open **distributed** **traces** filtered by **service** **name** and **route**. Situation: constraints; Task: reliability goal; Action: design + metrics; Result: outage reduction o Close with **prevention**: **contract** tests for **clients**, **SLO** alerts on **client-side** **error** rates, **game** days that **kill** **pods** during **business** hours in **staging**, and **lint** rules banning **blocking** calls on **reactor** threads.

followUps.question: How do you **roll out** a policy change here without a **retry** **storm**?
followUps.answer: Use **feature** **flags**, **canary** **deployments**, **tight** **retry** **budgets** on **non-idempotent** calls, and **verify** with **load** tests that **match** **production** **fan-out**. Situation: constraints; Task: reliability goal; Action: design + metrics; Result: outage r Communicate **executive**-friendly **impact**: **customer** **minutes** degraded, **error** **budget** burn, **mitigation** **ETA**, and **post-incident** **themes** mapped to **roadmap** work. Reference **security**: **mTLS** **service** identity, **OAuth2** **scopes**, **audit** logs at **edge**, and why **secrets** never belong in **Feign** **URL** query strings.

conceptual.question: How to **disagree** **constructively** in **story**?
conceptual.answer: Data, alternatives, escalation path—no personal blame narrative. Contrast **happy** **path** docs with **partition** behavior: **split** **retries**, **half-open** **probes**, **degraded** **read** modes, and **idempotency** keys that prevent **duplicate** **writes**. Mention **verification**: **`curl -v`** through **gateway**, **`kubectl logs`**, **APM** **waterfall** **spans**, and **dashboards** that slice **p99** by **downstream** **service** **name**.

followUps.question: Production follow-up: which **dashboards** or **metrics** expose this for **System design case study**?
followUps.answer: Start with **client** **outbound** **histograms** and **resilience4j** **circuitbreaker** **state** **gauges**, then open **distributed** **traces** filtered by **service** **name** and **route**. Data, alternatives, escalation path—no personal blame narrative. Interviewers reward naming **metrics** (`http_client_requests_seconds`, `resilience4j_circuitbreaker_state`), **trace** correlation IDs, and **rollback** criteria rather than hand-wavy resilience claims. Tie answers to **Spring** reality: **Feign** + **LoadBalancer**, **WebClient** + **`ExchangeFilterFunction`**, **Resilience4j** registries, and **Micrometer** tags that make **on-call** searches fast.

followUps.question: How do you **roll out** a policy change here without a **retry** **storm**?
followUps.answer: Use **feature** **flags**, **canary** **deployments**, **tight** **retry** **budgets** on **non-idempotent** calls, and **verify** with **load** tests that **match** **production** **fan-out**. Data, alternatives, escalation path—no personal blame narrative. Mention **verification**: **`curl -v`** through **gateway**, **`kubectl logs`**, **APM** **waterfall** **spans**, and **dashboards** that slice **p99** by **downstream** **service** **name**. Senior answers separate **symptom** from **root** **cause**: **502** at **edge** can be **empty** **Endpoints**, **stale** **pool**, or **upstream** **panic**—each needs different **first** **commands**.

conceptual.question: **Trade-off** **triangle** **phrasing**?
conceptual.answer: Latency vs cost vs consistency—pick two explicitly for the scenario. Close with **prevention**: **contract** tests for **clients**, **SLO** alerts on **client-side** **error** rates, **game** days that **kill** **pods** during **business** hours in **staging**, and **lint** rules banning **blocking** calls on **reactor** threads. When **Kubernetes** is involved, cite **readiness**, **HPA**, **NetworkPolicy**, and **Service** **DNS** as factors that change **latency** independent of **Java** **code**.

For **gateway** and **service** stacks, emphasize **timeout** ordering (**outer > inner**), **bulkheads** per **tenant**, and **JWT** **cache** **TTL** during **issuer** rotations.

seniorScenario.question: **Incident:** **System Design Case Study and Behavioral** deploy correlates with **5xx** spike.
seniorScenario.answer:
**(1) Immediate response** Freeze risky changes; open **SLO** dashboard.

**(2) Root cause** **Config** regression or **dependency** **saturation**.

**(3) Fix** Rollback or **toggle** **flag**; restore **SLO** then root-cause.

**(4) Prevention** Add **canary** gate and **diff** **alerts** on **golden** metrics. Close with **prevention**: **contract** tests for **clients**, **SLO** alerts on **client-side** **error** rates, **game** days that **kill** **pods** during **business** hours in **staging**, and **lint** rules banning **blocking** calls on **reactor** threads. When **Kubernetes** is involved, cite **readiness**, **HPA**, **NetworkPolicy**, and **Service** **DNS** as factors that change **latency** independent of **Java** **code**. For **gateway** and **service** stacks, emphasize **timeout** ordering (**outer > inner**), **bulkheads** per **tenant**, and **JWT** **cache** **TTL** during **issuer** rotations. Communicate **executive**-friendly **impact**: **customer** **minutes** degraded, **error** **budget** burn, **mitigation** **ETA**, and **post-incident** **themes** mapped to **roadmap** work. Reference **security**: **mTLS** **service** identity, **OAuth2** **scopes**, **audit** logs at **edge**, and why **secrets** never belong in **Feign** **URL** query strings.

followUps.question: How do you **communicate** **impact** and **ETA** during this incident?
followUps.answer: Share **SLO** **burn**, **customer** **regions** affected, **mitigation** steps in flight, and **explicit** unknowns. Avoid **blaming** **users**; focus on **measurable** recovery criteria. Explain **second-order** effects: **retry** amplification, **queue** growth, **thread** **pool** exhaustion, and **cost** of extra **hops** when **edge** dependencies like **Redis** rate-limit stores fail. Close with **prevention**: **contract** tests for **clients**, **SLO** alerts on **client-side** **error** rates, **game** days that **kill** **pods** during **business** hours in **staging**, and **lint** rules banning **blocking** calls on **reactor** threads.

sections.title: Cheatsheet
sections.content:
| Piece | Recall |
|---|---|
| STAR | structure |
| Metric | prove impact |
| Trade-off | name 3 options |
| Risk | honest caveats |
| Rollback | always plan |
| SLO | customer lens |
| ADR | document why |
| Stakeholder | plain English |
| Follow-up | pause think |
| Close | recap decision |
